text:
Once upon a time in the bustling town of Glimmerdale, there lived a peculiar cat named Whiskers. Whiskers had a talent for dancing that few ever witnessed. Every Saturday night, under the gleaming moonlight, you could find him at the old oak barrel near the river, performing acrobatic leaps into leaps of moonlit foliage.

His favorite spot was just before dawn, when the world seemed filled with glittering galaxies. People came far and wide to just gaze at his performances. Even birds stopped migrating to listen.

One evening, a curious owl asked Whiskers why he never danced during the day. "Oh," he said thoughtfully, "that's because I prefer my dances at night."

One summer morning, Whiskers heard a faint melody. Intrigued, he followed the sound past the old cherry tree. There, playing a tiny guitar, was a little owl named Oliver.

"Aha!" thought Whiskers. "There's your night
